briefing.opening: Good morning. I will now begin the climate briefing for May 11, 2026.

sections.title: ⚠️ Deepening Climate Change Threats and Urgent Response Challenges
sections.content: News reported today once again reminds us that climate change poses a serious global threat. Domestically, an increase in pests and diseases in the Gyeongbuk region due to climate change has been reported [2, 3], and the review of the IPCC's draft report on 'Climate Change and Cities' has begun, drawing attention to the challenges it poses for South Korean urban policy [10]. Notably, in Europe, a warning has been issued that inequality increases the mortality rate from climate disasters, leading to an additional 100,000 deaths annually [18], while the UK's tea industry and Maine's wild blueberry farms also face threats from changing growing seasons and altered flavors due to climate change [144, 145, 149, 150, 152, 158, 160, 161, 163]. These warnings clearly demonstrate that responding to the climate crisis extends beyond simple environmental issues to encompass socioeconomic inequality and security concerns [162].

sections.title: 🌱 Accelerating Renewable Energy Transition for Carbon Neutrality
sections.content: Meanwhile, efforts to transition to renewable energy for achieving carbon neutrality are actively continuing both domestically and internationally. KT has signed a Power Purchase Agreement (PPA) with Tokyo Electron Korea to supply up to 50MW of renewable energy, accelerating carbon neutrality in the semiconductor industry [22, 24, 25, 36, 45, 46, 56, 60, 61, 63, 66, 72, 74, 75, 77, 79, 80, 81, 82, 83, 86, 90, 92, 95, 96, 97, 100, 101, 102, 105, 108, 112, 114, 124, 125, 128, 131, 132, 133, 134, 136]. Shinsung E&G has declared a renewed leap in its renewable energy business, aiming to restore its competitiveness in solar panel manufacturing [70, 87, 88, 104, 106, 107, 109], and Kolon Benit has registered as a renewable energy electricity supplier, entering the direct PPA market and expecting to play a pivotal role in assisting companies with their ESG management [78, 84, 85, 103, 111, 115, 116, 119, 130]. Furthermore, Shinhan Bank is expanding global renewable energy business cooperation with companies like Hyosung Heavy Industries, strengthening financial support [71, 76, 89, 91, 94, 98, 99, 110, 118, 120, 122, 126, 127, 129, 140, 141, 142], and KORAIL has launched solar power and E-train projects with the goal of achieving RE100 by 2050 [93], demonstrating clear movements towards renewable energy transition across all industrial sectors.

sections.title: 🇰🇷 Efforts to Adapt to Climate Change and Expand Education
sections.content: Efforts to adapt to climate change and expand education are also actively underway across various regions in South Korea. Jeju Island is accelerating the development of new citrus varieties to respond to rapidly changing climate and consumption trends, with plans to cultivate an additional 3 to 9 new varieties by 2030 [7, 8, 11, 143]. Furthermore, research is being conducted on how heat-tolerant corals can help coral reefs adapt to climate change [147]. Domestically, elementary school students have demonstrated wise activities in response to climate change through a 'Zero Food Waste' campaign and the use of personal water bottles [1], and efforts to establish climate education hubs and nurture talent for future generations are being strengthened, including the opening of the Nowon-gu Carbon Neutrality Experience Center [16, 19, 20, 21, 34, 37, 39, 50, 53, 59] and the Daejeon Regional Meteorological Administration's climate crisis response contest for elementary school students [9] [12, 14, 15, 52]. The Incheon Institute's proposal for an 'Incheon-type Public Disaster Prevention Model' [13] and Busan City's declaration to leap forward as a global green city through 'carbon-neutral buildings' [54] present concrete practical measures for local communities to adapt to climate change.

sections.title: 📰 Activation of Climate Policy and Social Discourse
sections.content: Social discussions for improving climate policy and legal frameworks are also actively progressing. Following the Constitutional Court's unconstitutional ruling, discussions on amending the Carbon Neutrality Framework Act have proceeded, with citizens opting for aggressive carbon reduction [48]. The Korea Forest Service is strengthening carbon neutrality cooperation with local governments to jointly respond to achieving the 2035 National Determined Contribution (NDC) [43, 64], and discussions such as the forum on amending the Carbon Neutrality Act for the future [26] and the opinion that the K-Steel Act lacks specific support measures for achieving net-zero [51] suggest the need for policy improvement. Various stakeholders are participating in discussions and seeking practical measures for the transition to a carbon-neutral society, including the feed industry's establishment of carbon neutrality implementation plans for livestock farming [31] and the Ulsan Port Authority and Energy Security and Environment Association's forum on carbon-neutral energy logistics hub strategy [58]. In particular, with the passage of the Agri-PV Act, a new market worth 10GW annually is expected to open, but challenges regarding persistent entry barriers are also being raised [47, 123].

briefing.closing: Today's briefing has highlighted the multifaceted expansion of renewable energy transition and climate change adaptation efforts, both domestically and internationally, amidst the escalating threat of climate change. We hope these efforts will lead to tangible results and contribute to overcoming the climate crisis through stronger policy support. This concludes the climate briefing for May 11, 2026.
